metadata.title: Local Union Support Hub
metadata.description: Professional tools for any local union — comms, grievances, and more

home.title: Local Union Support Hub
home.subtitle: Professional guides and tools for volunteer union communicators — any local, any union
home.privacyNote: All processing happens on your device. No data is sent to any server.

brandKit.title: Brand Kit Manager
brandKit.description: Manage, export, and import your local branding settings

logo.title: Logo
logo.description: Use the official CAAT OPSEU logo or upload your local union logo (PNG, JPEG, WebP, or SVG).
logo.uploadHint: Accepts PNG, JPEG, WebP, or SVG up to 10 MB.

consent.title: Member photo consent
consent.description: Before using a member's photo in social media graphics, confirm:
consent.item1: You have written or verbal consent from the member
consent.item2: The member understands the photo will be posted publicly
consent.item3: The photo does not contain confidential workplace information
consent.confirm: I confirm consent has been obtained

contrast.fail: Fails WCAG AA contrast — adjust colours for accessibility

crisisGuide.title: Strike & Crisis Comms Guide
crisisGuide.subtitle: High-level guidance for CAAT OPSEU locals during strikes, bargaining, and workplace crises
crisisGuide.intro: OPSEU/SEFPO provides sector-level bargaining support, but locals run their own member communications — especially on social media. This guide helps volunteer comms leads stay coordinated, lawful, and member-centred when stakes are high. Always defer to your local executive, OPSEU servicing rep, and any active strike or bargaining protocols.

strike.title: Strike action
items: Confirm all public messaging with your local president and OPSEU servicing rep before posting
Designate one or two spokespersons — members and media should hear one consistent local voice
Post daily picket schedules, gate locations, and safety reminders using branded graphics from this toolbox
Share solidarity and member stories with consent — never post photos without permission
Use OPSEU blue branding; follow CAAT Support campaign materials when coordinating with division
Do not share negotiation details, member home addresses, or internal strategy on public channels
Direct questions about pay, duties, and return-to-work to the local strike committee — not comment threads

bargaining.title: Bargaining and countdown
items: Build momentum with factual updates — job security, workload, wages, and member priorities
Use consistent hashtags and graphics so members recognize official local posts
Encourage members to wear OPSEU blue and attend information meetings — post times and locations early
Share only what the bargaining team has approved for public release
Remind members to avoid debating management claims in public comment sections
Link to OPSEU/CAAT bargaining pages when available rather than paraphrasing from memory

layoffs.title: Layoffs and restructuring
items: Acknowledge member anxiety — be empathetic, factual, and calm
Direct affected members to stewards, OPSEU servicing reps, and EAP resources
Do not speculate about outcomes — share only confirmed local and employer statements
Use quote cards from elected leaders to show a unified local voice
Document management communications offline for grievance and consultation records
Avoid naming individuals at risk unless they have asked to be identified publicly

management.title: Management pushback and misinformation
items: Respond with facts and collective agreement language — never personal attacks
If management posts misinformation, publish one brief correction and pin it if needed
Escalate harassment, threats, or surveillance concerns to your OPSEU rep immediately
Do not engage in prolonged public arguments with management accounts
Keep sensitive member issues in steward channels, not public posts
Screenshot problematic employer posts for the local executive and servicing rep

escalation.title: When to escalate
items: Media inquiries — route to your designated spokesperson or OPSEU communications
Legal threats, injunctions, or employer letters about conduct — executive and servicing rep immediately
Member safety incidents on the line or in the workplace — follow local emergency protocols first
Leaked confidential documents or suspected bad-faith bargaining — do not post; alert leadership
Viral misinformation about your local — coordinate a single correction rather than multiple replies

tools.title: Toolbox resources for crisis comms
tools.description: Use these on-device tools to keep visuals consistent when your local is under pressure:
items: Brand Kit — local number, colours, and logo ready for fast graphics
Flyer Maker and Quote Card — leadership statements and picket updates
Graphic Maker — member spotlights with consent flow
Captions library — pre-written solidarity and bargaining posts

footer.madeBy: Built for union locals by union volunteers
footer.privacy: Your data stays on your device

commitment.title: Our commitment
commitment.body: The Local Union Support Hub is committed to ensuring digital accessibility for all members, including those with disabilities. We aim to conform to the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 Level AA and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA).

features.title: Accessibility features
features.semanticHtml: Semantic HTML with proper heading hierarchy
features.focusIndicators: Visible focus indicators on all interactive elements
features.contrast: Colour contrast checked against WCAG AA standards
features.reducedMotion: Reduced motion support via system settings and user preference
features.displaySettings: Adjustable font size, high contrast, and reduced motion settings
features.skipLink: Skip link to jump directly to main content
features.altTextTool: Alt-Text Assistant tool to help create accessible social media posts

limitations.title: Known limitations
limitations.body: Canvas-based image preview areas may not be fully accessible to screen reader users and may not reflect high-contrast display settings. All inputs that control the preview are accessible via standard form controls. Exported images should include alt-text added via the Alt-Text Assistant.

feedback.title: Feedback
feedback.body: If you encounter accessibility barriers while using this hub, please contact your local communications chair. We welcome feedback and are committed to continuous improvement.

display.title: Display settings
display.intro: Customize how the site looks and moves. Your choices are saved on this device.
display.fontSizeDescription: Choose a comfortable reading size. All text scales proportionally.
display.highContrastDescription: Stronger text and border contrast for easier reading.
display.reducedMotionDescription: Minimize animations and transitions, regardless of system settings.
display.previewText: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. Your local union deserves tools that work for everyone.

hub.title: Officer Hub
hub.loginSubtitle: Sign in to access grievance tracking and local tools
hub.demoHint: Demo: president@local243.ca / demo123 or solo@example.ca / demo123
hub.mfaSubtitle: Multi-factor authentication is required for confidential modules
hub.mfaError: Invalid code — enter any 6 digits in dev mode

modules.commsDesc: Public social media tools and guides

grievance.title: Grievance Tracking
grievance.subtitle: Track cases, deadlines, and officer notes
grievance.empty: No grievances yet. Create one to get started.
grievance.emailDraftWarning: Drafts only — review and send manually. Never auto-send.
grievance.memberPseudonymHint: Optional — use a pseudonym instead of real name

bumping.title: College Bumping
bumping.subtitle: Stability committee position comparison and decision log
bumping.disclaimer: Checklists assist committee process — not legal advice. Follow your collective agreement.
bumping.pdfError: Could not read PDF — paste text manually
bumping.compareHint: Differences highlighted — duties, qualifications, seniority markers
bumping.decisionHint: Record outcome for local records — committee decides, not software
